entities.name: PLANT VIRUS DISEASES
entities.description: Diseases caused by viruses in plants requiring precise identification for management

entities.name: SEROLOGY
entities.description: Sensitive serological methods used for the detection and diagnosis of viruses in plant tissues

entities.name: ELECTRON MICROSCOPY
entities.description: Transmission electron microscopy used to detect viruses in plant tissues at low concentrations

entities.name: VIRUSES
entities.description: Pathogenic agents that cause viral diseases in plants

entities.name: VISUAL SYMPTOMS
entities.description: External manifestations of diseases used for early identification but prone to confusion

entities.name: HOST RANGE
entities.description: The range of host plants a virus can infect, used in early disease reports

entities.name: SPIROPLASMA
entities.description: Organisms whose associated diseases can be confused with viral diseases

entities.name: RICKETSIAE
entities.description: Organisms whose associated diseases can be confused with viral diseases

entities.name: MYCOPLASMA-LIKE ORGANISMS
entities.description: Organisms whose associated diseases can be confused with viral diseases

entities.name: NUTRIENT DEFICIENCY
entities.description: A state of lacking nutrients that produces symptoms similar to viral diseases

entities.name: GENOTYPE
entities.description: The genetic makeup of a plant which influences the external symptoms of viral diseases

entities.name: PLANT AGE
entities.description: The developmental stage of a plant which influences the external symptoms of viral diseases

entities.name: ENVIRONMENT
entities.description: External surroundings that influence the external symptoms of viral diseases

entities.name: VIRUS STRAIN
entities.description: Specific variants of a virus that influence the external symptoms produced
